helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function convert_points_to_homogeneous at 0x7f08890c2440>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function denormalize_pixel_coordinates3d at 0x7f08890c32e0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function denormalize_points_with_intrinsics at 0x7f08890c3760>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function quaternion_to_axis_angle at 0x7f08890c2b90>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function normalize_quaternion at 0x7f08890c2a70>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function Rt_to_matrix4x4 at 0x7f08890c37f0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function camtoworld_to_worldtocam_Rt at 0x7f08890c3b50>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function camtoworld_vision_to_graphics_Rt at 0x7f08890c3ac0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.feature.responses.BlobDoGSingle'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <function distort_points at 0x7f48b83880d0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function conv_soft_argmax2d at 0x7f8d20cd20e0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function spatial_softmax2d at 0x7f8d20cd1360>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function nms2d at 0x7f8d20cd1900>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.geometry.subpix.spatial_soft_argmax.ConvQuadInterp3d'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <function find_homography_dlt_iterated at 0x7f9ec1e06ef0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.feature.orientation.LAFOrienter'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.

        ----

            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,

object = <function bbox_generator at 0x7f854615bb50>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function bbox_to_mask3d at 0x7f854615bac0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def test_transform_bbox(target_framework, mode, backend_compile):

object = <function transform_bbox at 0x7f854615bc70>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function box_blur at 0x7f5837091360>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function joint_bilateral_blur at 0x7f5837090ee0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.

        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function sobel at 0x7f5837092dd0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function filter2d at 0x7f58370915a0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function get_gaussian_erf_kernel1d at 0x7f5837090160>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function get_hanning_kernel2d at 0x7f5837090700>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.filters.canny.Canny'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

__________________________________________________________________________________ test_RANSAC[tensorflow-s2s-False] ___________________________________________________________________________________

target_framework = 'tensorflow', mode = 's2s', backend_compile = False

    def test_RANSAC(target_framework, mode, backend_compile):
        print("kornia.geometry.ransac.RANSAC")
    
        if backend_compile:
            pytest.skip()
    
        # Initialize RANSAC with default parameters

object = <class 'kornia.geometry.ransac.RANSAC'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function distance_transform at 0x7ff732a8cee0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.

        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

        The translated object.

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function erosion at 0x7f113cdd3f40>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function gradient at 0x7f113cdf0160>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def transpile(

        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,

object = <function gftt_response at 0x7f4ab4f4fb50>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function dog_response at 0x7f4ab4f4fc70>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function match_nn at 0x7f4ab4fa8700>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def test_extract_patches_simple(target_framework, mode, backend_compile):

helpers.py:246:

object = <function extract_patches_simple at 0x7f4ab4f03e20>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function laf_to_boundary_points at 0x7f4ab4f03b50>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function ellipse_to_laf at 0x7f4ab4f03ac0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function rgb_to_grayscale at 0x7f5544637370>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function bgr_to_rgb at 0x7f5544636f80>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function bgr_to_rgba at 0x7f55446370a0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source
